content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Frank J. DeMarco 
County Counsel 
County of Siskiyou 
P.O. Box 659 
Yreka, CA 
96097 
Dear Mr. DeMarco: 
Technical AnI .... n" 
(916) 322-5662 
Administration 
322-5660 
May II, 1984 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-ll3 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of the 
Siskiyou County Airport Director. 
The Director owns an airplane 
hangar at the Scott Valley Airport, a County owned airport. 
He 
is leaving the Scott Valley area and will no longer need the 
hangar. 
He has offered to sell the hangar to the County for 
approximately $1,000. 
You have asked whether this creates any 
conflict of interest. 
Under the Political Reform Act, a public official cannot 
make or participate in any governmental decision which will 
materially affect his financial interests. 
Government Code 
Sections 87100, 87103. 
Thus the Director may not participate in 
the County decision on the purchase of his own airplane hangar. 
The Director is not prohibited by the Political Reform Act 
from selling his hangar to the County, so long as he 
disqualifies himself trom participating in the County's decision 
in any way, including making recommendations to the County 
Administrator. 
However, you may want to look at Government Code 
Section 1090 to decide whether he would have a problem with this 
sale under any other provision of law. 
If I can be of any further help in this matter, do not 
hesitate to call. 
Very truly yours, 
General

FRANi1r:. J. D6MARCO 
COUNTY COUNSEL 
WILLIAM P. STANLEY 
ASST. COUNTY COUNSEL 
OFFICE OF 
COUNTY COUNSEL 
COUNTY OF SISKIYOU 
304 LANE STREET -
P,O. BOx 859 
YREKA, CALIFORNIA 96097 
April 24, 1984 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Request for advice 
Dear Commission Members: 
TELEPHONES 842-3531, Ex. 85 
842-4421 
This letter is written on behalf of the Siskiyou County 
Administrator, the Siskiyou County Airport Director, and the 
Siskiyou County Board of Supervisors seeking your advice with 
regard to whether a conflict of interest would exist concerning 
the purchase by the County from the Siskiyou County Airport 
Director of his personal airplane hangar situated at the Scott 
Valley Airport which is a County-owned airport. 
The hangar 
is situate on County-owned property and is valued at approximately 
$1,000. 
This hangar, in addition to being able to house an air-
plane, houses the County well and airport lighting system for the 
Scott Valley Airport. 
The Airport Director will no longer be 
residing in the Scott Valley area and does not need the hangar 
in order to house his airplane. 
He has offered to sell the hangar 
to the County for approximately $1,000 and the County Administrator 
has recommended, if legally appropriate, for the County to purchase 
the hangar since it does serve to house the County well and airport 
lighting system. 
Your advice with regard to the foregoing would be greatly 
appreciated. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated courtesy. 
Very truly yours, 
OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL

County Counsel

sections.question: Whether a conflict of interest would exist concerning the purchase by the County from the Siskiyou County Airport Director of his personal airplane hangar situated at the Scott Valley Airport which is a County-owned airport. The hangar is situated on County-owned property and is valued at approximately $1,000. The Airport Director will no longer be residing in the Scott Valley area and does not need the hangar in order to house his airplane. He has offered to sell the hangar to the County for approximately $1,000 and the County Administrator has recommended, if legally appropriate, for the County to purchase the hangar since it does serve to house the County well and airport lighting system.
sections.conclusion: Under the Political Reform Act, a public official cannot make or participate in any governmental decision which will materially affect his financial interests. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103. Thus the Director may not participate in the County decision on the purchase of his own airplane hangar. The Director is not prohibited by the Political Reform Act from selling his hangar to the County, so long as he disqualifies himself from participating in the County's decision in any way, including making recommendations to the County Administrator. However, you may want to look at Government Code Section 1090 to decide whether he would have a problem with this sale under any other provision of law.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a County Airport Director may sell his personal airplane hangar located on County-owned property to the County without creating a conflict of interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Airport Director may sell the hangar to the County under the Political Reform Act provided he completely disqualifies himself from any participation in the County's decision to purchase it. The FPPC also recommends examining Government Code Section 1090 for potential conflicts under other state law provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document includes both incoming request letter (dated April 24, 1984) from County Counsel and FPPC response letter (dated May 11, 1984). FPPC response provides concrete legal advice based on Political Reform Act provisions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a conflict of interest would exist concerning the purchase by the County from the Siskiyou County Airport Director of his personal airplane hangar situated at the Scott Valley Airport which is a County-owned airport. The hangar is situated on County-owned property and is valued at approximately $1,000. The Airport Director will no longer be residing in the Scott Valley area and does not need the hangar in order to house his airplane. He has offered to sell the hangar to the County for approximately $1,000 and the County Administrator has recommended, if legally appropriate, for the County to purchase the hangar since it does serve to house the County well and airport lighting system.

CONCLUSION: Under the Political Reform Act, a public official cannot make or participate in any governmental decision which will materially affect his financial interests. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103. Thus the Director may not participate in the County decision on the purchase of his own airplane hangar. The Director is not prohibited by the Political Reform Act from selling his hangar to the County, so long as he disqualifies himself from participating in the County's decision in any way, including making recommendations to the County Administrator. However, you may want to look at Government Code Section 1090 to decide whether he would have a problem with this sale under any other provision of law.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that a County Airport Director may sell his personal airplane hangar to the County without violating the Political Reform Act, provided he disqualifies himself from participating in the County's purchase decision, and recommended examination of Government Code Section 1090 for other potential conflicts.
